citation: Lessiter, J., Freeman, J., Keogh, E., & Davidoff, J. (2001). A cross-media presence questionnaire: The ITC Sense of Presence Inventory. Presence: Teleoperators and Virtual Environments, 10(3), 282-297.

PPS.label: Physical Space
PPS.description: Sense of being physically located in the mediated environment.
sample_items: I felt I was visiting the places in the presentation.
It seemed as though I actually took part in the action of the presentation.
I felt as though I was physically present in the environment shown.
The presentation made me feel as if I was somewhere else.
I felt I could have reached out and touched things in the presentation.
PPS.scoring: Sum of item scores (after reversals); divide by item count for mean. Range 1-5.
PPS.higher_score_means: Stronger sense of physical co-presence in virtual space.

ENG.label: Engagement
ENG.description: Degree of attention, involvement, and absorption in the VE.
sample_items: I found the presentation to be attention-grabbing.
I was so involved in the presentation that I lost track of time.
The presentation captured my attention.
I found myself wanting to interact with the environment.
The presentation was absorbing.
ENG.scoring: Sum of item scores (after reversals); divide by item count for mean. Range 1-5.

ECO.label: Ecological Validity / Naturalness
ECO.description: Perceived realism and naturalness of the virtual environment.
sample_items: The environment in the presentation seemed natural.
The way things moved in the presentation seemed natural.
Objects in the presentation looked real.
The sounds in the presentation were realistic.
The presentation seemed like a real place.
ECO.scoring: Sum of item scores (after reversals); divide by item count for mean. Range 1-5.

NAU.label: Negative Effects (Nausea / Discomfort)
NAU.description: Adverse physiological and psychological effects during exposure.
sample_items: I felt sick during the presentation.
I felt dizzy during the presentation.
I felt disoriented during the presentation.
I experienced discomfort during the presentation.
I felt nauseous during the presentation.
NAU.scoring: Sum of item scores; divide by item count for mean. Range 1-5.
NAU.note: Excluded from positive presence composite; reported separately.

composite_score.method: Mean of PPS, ENG, and ECO subscale means. NAU reported separately.

reverse_coding_procedure: For reverse-coded items: reversed_score = (max_scale + 1) - raw_score (i.e., 6 - raw for 1-5 scale)

cross_media_validation.notes: Designed for cross-media comparison; validated across 5 media types.

notes: 44 items is longer than IPQ; consider short-form (22-item) for repeated measures.
NAU subscale can serve as a cybersickness severity index.
ECO subscale uniquely captures perceptual realism vs. IPQ REAL subscale.
PPS most closely parallels IPQ SP subscale; ENG parallels INV.
Validated for cross-media use — suitable for comparing HMD vs. non-HMD conditions.
